## PAGE 01177: Working with NCV-5 typical table

- page_id: `136721360`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721360/Working+with+NCV-5+typical+table

- labels: []

628010594

##### Creating NCV-5 typical table

The context of the NCV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the NCV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the NCV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the NCV-5 typical table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with NCV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].

###### emptyCreate a NCV-5 typical table

To create an empty NCV-5 typical table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a NCV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. An***Exhibits***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table

To remove Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more NCV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Exhibits***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a NCV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a NCV-5 typical table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a NCV-5 typical table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 typical table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

628015084

## PAGE 01178: Working with NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `136721430`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721430/Working+with+NSOV-3+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:07.690+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **NSOV-3****Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>NSOV-3 </strong><strong>Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 01179: Working with Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `136722007`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722007/Working+with+Operational+Activities+to+Capabilities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:54.304+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282484386

1282484407

1282484397

The Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix (Op-Tr)****shows the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

##### Creating Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

The Rows of this matrix are Operational Activities and the Columns are Capabilities.

To create an Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Operational Activities).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps To Capability relationship.

##### Mapping an Operational Activity to a Capability

To map an Operational Activity to a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282484385

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024x']

/></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721913 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01180: Working with Operational Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `136721913`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721913/Working+with+Operational+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-29T08:17:37.289+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1324985976

100.00002%

1324985979

1324985975

**On this page**

1324985977

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Operational Connectivity diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Connectivity and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Operational Connectivity . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_diagram.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Operational **Connectivity** and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x'].

An Operational Connectivity view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space=''] diagram.

##### Creating Operational Exchanges

Before creating Operational Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Operational Information, Resource Information, or Resource). The you can start creating Operational Exchanges.

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Connectivity diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select an Operational Performer and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Operational Exchange . Click another Operational Performer to connect. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the diagram palette, c lick **Operational Exchange** and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

##### Realizing Operational Exchanges on Associations

When you have Operational Performers connected with an Association, there are two ways to create operational exchanges:

- Opening Exchange Manager.
- Dragging the Exchange Items on an Association.

###### Opening the Exchange Manager

To create new Operational Exchange on the Association

1. Click the Association between Operational Performers.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click New Operational Exchange. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_op_exchange.png']
3. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
5. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once an Association Relationship is created between two structural elements, all existing exchanges between the elements are visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog.

To open an **Operational Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Selectan Association (also Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message) in the diagram pane.
2. In the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The Operational Exchange Manager dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Click to select or clear the check box in the Realize column to show (realize) or hide Operational Exchanges on the Association.

###### Dragging the Exchange Item on the Association

You can create the Operational Exchanges on the Association by dragging the Exchange Elements onto the Association. When you drop the Exchange Element on the Association, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the wizard, specify the needed information.

In the Operational Exchange drop-down list, the existing Operational Exchanges will be listed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721937 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01181: Working with Operational Connectivity Table

- page_id: `136721937`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721937/Working+with+Operational+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-29T08:16:43.836+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

833582449

833582451

833582450

An Operational Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Operational Performers. You can add new or existing Operational Exchange to fill in an Operational Connectivity Table.

##### Adding new Operational Exchanges

To add new Operational Exchange

1. In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Operational Exchanges

To add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Operational Performer, Receiving Operational Performer, and Implements cells are read-only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d51be05d-315c-499a-a8e0-a99a8c674523"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">833582449</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7dac587-5f13-4f02-b8f3-888650da3605"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">833582451</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32274288-8792-41e1-b3db-4159c0f3d82a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">833582450</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>An  Operational Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Operational Performers. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchange to fill in an Operational Connectivity Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the  Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Sending Operational Performer, Receiving <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Performer</span>, and Implements cells are read-only.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use</span> <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> that enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721943 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01182: Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `136721943`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721943/Working+with+Operational+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:48.917+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating an Operational Constraints Definition diagram

To create an Operational Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create Operational Performers.
2. Connect them using the Associations relationship.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.

An Operational Constraints Definition view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] diagram.

##### Creating an Operational Exchange in Operational Constraints Definition diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Constraints Definition diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of Operational Performers and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Creating an Operational Constraints Definition diagram</h3><p>To create an Operational Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Performers.</li><li>Connect them using the Associations relationship.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>An Operational Constraints Definition view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.</p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Internal Connectivity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><h3>Creating an Operational Exchange in Operational Constraints Definition diagram</h3><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Operational Exchange</strong></span> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of Operational Performers and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>. </li><li> <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.</span> </span></li></ul><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721941 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01183: Working with Operational Constraints table

- page_id: `136721941`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721941/Working+with+Operational+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:48.795+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Constraints table by adding a new Operational Constraint

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] and in the Select Subject Of Operational Constraint dialog, select constrained Operational Elements (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Operational Information, Operational Exchange).
3. In the **Rule** **Specification**cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.
4. Select the **Rule Kind** from one of the following:
  - Structural Assertion
  - Action Assertion
  - Derivation
  - Contract
  - Constraint
  - Guidance
  - Security Policy
  - Caveat

You can assign more than one Operational Constraint for one constraint.

To create an Operational Constraints table by adding a new Operational Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In Select Operational Constraint dialog, select Operational Constraints. The filled row is added in table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722002 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01184: Working with Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `136722002`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722002/Working+with+Operational+High+Level+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:53.091+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

To create an Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have Resource Taxonomy and Operational Taxonomy diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div class="wiki-content"><p>An Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram.</p><p>A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</p><p>To create an Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have Resource Taxonomy and Operational Taxonomy diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721928 space=UAF12P2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 01186: Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `136721928`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721928/Working+with+Operational+Internal+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-03-06T14:33:13.126+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

911131526

INLINE

911131528

INLINE

911131525

INLINE

**On this page**

911131527

INLINE

##### Creating Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

The owner of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram must be an Operational Performer.

To create an Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Operational Roles.
2. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors .
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors .

Create the Internal Connectivity Diagram from the Composite Structure element using any method which is most suitable for you:

- Right-click the Composite element in a Structure Diagram and Create Diagram.
- Right-click the Composite element in the Containment Tree and Create Diagram.
- Select the Internal Connectivity Diagram from the Smart Manipulator.

The diagram creation wizard is displayed, showing the hierarchical list of part properties owned by the selected composite element. All the elements or any subset can be included in the diagram.

##### Creating Operational Exchanges in Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector between a pair of Operational Roles, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector .
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

##### Showing or hiding realized Operational Exchanges

You can choose to either show or hide the Operational Exchange Item on the element realizing Operational Exchanges.

To show/hide the realized Operational Exchange Items

1. On the diagram, click the element for which you want to show or hide the realized exchange.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar :
  1. If theOperational Exchange Item is not displayed , the tooltip for the button is [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Show Realized Operational Exchanges . Click it to hide the Operational ExchangeItem.
  2. If the Operational ExchangeItem is displayed , the tooltip for the button is[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Hide RealizedOperational**Exchanges** . Click it to hide the Operational ExchangeItem. 
If theelement realizes only one Operational Exchange, the Operational ExchangeItem is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.
3. If the element realizes multiple Operational Exchanges, the Operational Exchange Items dialog opens. In the dialog, do the following:
  1. Select the check-boxes for the Operational Exchanges whose Operational Exchange Item you want to be displayed.
  2. Clear the check-boxes for the Operational Exchanges whose Operational ExchangeItem you want to be hidden. To select all check boxes, click the**Select All**button. To clear all check boxes, click the**Clear All**button.
  3. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='show_operational_exchanges.png']

1912134947

INLINE

**Learn more**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space=''].** You cancheck the consistency of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram with Operational Activities, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721951 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01187: Working with Operational Parametric diagram

- page_id: `136721951`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721951/Working+with+Operational+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:49.445+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Parameters
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

495805059

495805061

495805060

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024x']to constrain the properties of another Operational Performer. It contains[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024x']and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Operational Roles or Measurements) from within that internal Operational Performer context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

495805058

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It contains </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Operational Roles or Measurements) from within that internal Operational Performer context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.</span></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7bc1f2b-cbc9-40c5-a168-836e1ff80e4c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">495805058</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 01188: Working with Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `136722009`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722009/Working+with+Operational+Performers+to+Capabilities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-06-04T08:06:41.309+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix (Op-Tr)****displays the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational performers that those capabilities support.

##### Creating Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

The Rows of this matrix are Operational Performers, Operational Architecturesand their specializations, andthe Columns areCapabilities.

To create the Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Operational Performers, Operational Architectures).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] Refresh button.

Operational Performers and Operational Architectures map to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

##### Mapping an Operational Performer to a Capability

Operational Performers and Operational Architectures map to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To map Operational Performers to a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721956 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01189: Working with Operational Process Flow diagram

- page_id: `136721956`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721956/Working+with+Operational+Process+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:49.850+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1368826051

1368826053

1368826052

Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] must be created first.

To create an Operational Process Flow diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click Cancel in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Operational Performer or Operational Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Operational Activity Actions or drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Operational Activity Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Operational Process Flow diagram with the top element -*Distress Signal Handling*as a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The OperationalProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Operational Activity Action or Operational Action. In both cases, you can create an inner OperationalProcess Flow diagram for an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create an OperationalProcess Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Operational Activity Action or Operational Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Operational Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new OperationalProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Operational Activity Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Operational Performer and Operational Activity (the behavior of the Operational Action). Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Operational Activity Action.

1368826050

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlane' space='EAIP']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721955 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01190: Working with Operational Processes diagram

- page_id: `136721955`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721955/Working+with+Operational+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:49.727+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Processes diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create Operational Performers or reuse them from Operational Taxonomy view (recommended).
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an Operational Processes diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create Operational Performers or reuse them from Operational Taxonomy view (recommended).</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721938 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01191: Working with Operational Role-based Connectivity Table

- page_id: `136721938`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721938/Working+with+Operational+Role-based+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-29T08:16:31.846+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Performers). You can add anexisting Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table.

To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table

1. In the Operational Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector and click **+** button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Performers). You can add <span class="confluence-link">an </span><span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Operational Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48f021b3-2cf1-43b8-8ca7-31a54cac7bdd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector and click <strong>+</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721961 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01192: Working with Operational Sequences diagram

- page_id: `136721961`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721961/Working+with+Operational+Sequences+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:50.405+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Sequences
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Sequences diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Message between a pair of two Lifelines of Operational Roles and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, select the Operational Message.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721969 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01193: Working with Operational Structure diagram

- page_id: `136721969`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721969/Working+with+Operational+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:51.287+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

878769659

878769661

878769658

**On this page**

878769660

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Operational Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Operational Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Operational Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x'].

##### Connecting the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Operational Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.select_aggregation.png']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Relationship Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x']** dialog, select **Association.** If Operational Performers were related using the Operational Association relationship, select the **Operational Association** also.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024x'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024x'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

557474212

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MD2024x']

ri:filename="create_operational_structure_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Operational <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Structure</span> </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span><span>. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.</span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="331" ac:width="560"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_op_performer.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Operational Performer and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="473" ac:width="365"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_op_performer_containment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="313" ac:width="797"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_op_performer.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7d01530-058c-4324-a12b-1096609019f2"><ac:rich-text-body>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the Operational Performers</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op_composition_pallet.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="295" ac:width="847"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_structure_draw_composition.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Operational Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="316" ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.information_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Association</strong> and choose <strong>Aggregation</strong></span>.</p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="221" ac:width="383"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.select_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="126" ac:width="501"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.select_aggregation_example.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="279" ac:width="502"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.change_role_name.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: left;">To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="667" ac:width="500"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.set_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><h3 style="text-align: left;">Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Relationship Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d13e10d-1e94-4838-bbd0-5cc2c6d0a234"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If Operational Performers were related using the Operational Association relationship, select the <strong>Operational Association</strong> also.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="887" ac:width="727"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.compartments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.attributes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fd4c464-ed89-4754-b3b4-2f0c0451e567"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="773" ac:width="747"><ri:attachment ri:filename="op.str.display_related_nested.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83b3b0da-5d2f-42f8-90c0-498c9657b31a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">557474212</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721991 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01194: Working with Operational Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `136721991`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721991/Working+with+Operational+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:52.525+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Operational > Operational Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

911370602

911370604

478104274

**On this page**

911370603

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Operational Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Operational Taxonomy**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Operational Taxonomy**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x'].

##### Connect the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

- An Operational Taxonomy view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.
- The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aea71697-aa97-463b-bc26-11eff3d2fdcb"><ac:parameter
````
