## PAGE 01211: Working with Personnel Parametric diagram

- page_id: `136722125`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722125/Working+with+Personnel+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:59.826+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Parameters
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

496255768

496255770

496255769

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024x'] to constrain the properties of another Organizational Resources (e.g. Organization, Post, Person). It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024x'] and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Resource Role or Measurements) from within that internal Organizational Resources context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

496255767

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc8ffbb9-0a77-4fe2-85cd-25ed0a7bfe7d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496255768</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5897bd3c-9d02-4f80-8163-050f5fc3b998"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496255770</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20431d70-52bb-4f6d-8dfa-dd82e1ed7518"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496255769</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /></ac:link> to constrain the properties of another Organizational Resources (e.g. Organization, Post, Person). It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Resource Role or Measurements) from within that internal Organizational Resources context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6044e446-b71f-4a29-ab7c-d6c9b699e33c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496255767</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722129 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01212: Working with Personnel Processes diagram

- page_id: `136722129`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722129/Working+with+Personnel+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:00.044+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the Personnel Processes diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Processes' space='UAF12P2024x']Operational Processes and PersonnelStructure diagrams should be created first.

To create a Personnel Processes diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) resources from Personnel Structure, Personnel Taxonomy diagrams.
3. Use Is Capable To Perform r elationship to connect the resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Processes' space='UAF12P2024x']Operational Processes diagrams.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f52e3b83-f45c-4cd2-ac62-09f325eff963"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the Personnel Processes diagram, the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Processes" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Operational Processes</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> and Personnel<span class="confluence-link"> Structure</span> diagrams should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a Personnel Processes diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) resources from<span class="confluence-link"> Personnel Structure, Personnel Taxonomy diagrams.</span></li><li>Use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Is Capable To Perform r</span>elationship to connect the resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Processes" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Operational Processes</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> diagrams.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722130 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01213: Working with Personnel Processes Flow diagram

- page_id: `136722130`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722130/Working+with+Personnel+Processes+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:00.129+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1429120680

1429120701

1462901399

**On this page**

1429120691

##### Creating Personnel Processes Flow diagram

Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] must be created first.

To create a Personnel Process Flow diagram for Function Action flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Organizational Resource or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click **Cancel**in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Organizational Resource or Resource Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Function Action or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Function Action wit h Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Personnel Process Flow diagram with the top element -*Organization UK SAR Organization*as a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Personnel Process Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Function Action or Resource Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Personnel Process Flow diagram for a Function Action or Resource Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create a Personnel Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Function Action or Resource Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Function Action or Resource Action.
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Personnel Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Organizational Resource or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new Personnel Process Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Function Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Organizational Resource and Function (the behavior of the Function Action).

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Personnel Processes Flow diagram

To create a Resource Exchangein the Personnel Processes Flow diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and then click Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the ResourceExchangesdata.

1429120678

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f131d27a-50f9-46b5-93f0-bc5ef9a6ce9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1429120680</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="351e92a1-f781-4fd5-b719-1ef5ff173cbb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1429120701</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62a3ae35-6e93-447d-8679-88cf1a47255b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1462901399</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd9f2dea-5937-4ec9-9e3f-6a321e90a45d" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74538f65-ed24-44f1-9538-f351141f7409"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1429120691</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating Personnel Processes Flow diagram</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec96246b-6e29-4e70-a8d4-6b7ac218a871"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating this diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Processes diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Process diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create a Personnel Process Flow diagram for Function Action flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Organizational Resource or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="803bc96b-ef4e-42c8-9f4e-66d4d55bbc10"><ac:rich-text-body>If you click <strong>Cancel </strong>in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="02a4bcfc-c1a6-444f-a006-acd4953984da">Drag the Organizational Resource or Resource Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></li><li>Create Function Action or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect Function Action wit<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">h Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Display the possible Resource Exchanges on Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.</span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Let's say you have a defined structure and </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">create the Personnel Process Flow diagram with the top element - </span><em style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Organization UK SAR Organization</em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> as a Swimlane representative.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel_process_flow.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Processes Flow diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The P</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">ersonnel Process Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Function Action or Resource Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Personnel Process Flow diagram for a Function Action or Resource Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a Personnel Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Function Action or Resource Action</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Swimlane, select a needed Function Action or Resource Action.</li><li>In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Personnel Process Flow.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Organizational Resource or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel_process_flow_representatives.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Processes Flow diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16e29d1d-5e09-448e-9c17-28391584c765"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.</li><li>If you click <strong>Cancel</strong>, the empty diagram will be created.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />A new Personnel Process Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.</p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">When the Function Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Organizational Resource and Function (the behavior of the Function Action).</span><br /></span></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Creating Resource Exchange in Personnel Processes Flow diagram</span></h3><p>To create a <span class="confluence-link">Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>in the Personnel Processes Flow diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>by doing one of the following:<span> </span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click<span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange" ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Processes Flow diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">On the diagram palette, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange" ac:alt="Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Processes Flow diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>and then click Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:</span><ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Exchange</span>, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d12d1409-0664-4058-b64a-55b2789b31c8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd8eb90b-faef-480b-bacf-c6003f9fc179"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <strong><span class="confluence-link"> </span></strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchange</span> Manager</strong></span> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the <span class="confluence-link">Resource </span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span></span></span>data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96d278f4-3044-44bb-915d-f1af7de43cde"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1429120678</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722118 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 01214: Working with Personnel Role-based Connectivity Table

- page_id: `136722118`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722118/Working+with+Personnel+Role-based+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:59.367+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can fill in a Role-based Personnel Connectivity Table by adding new or existing Resource Exchanges.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges in a PersonnelRole-based Connectivity Table

To add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource **Exchange** dialog opens.
2. Click + button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:
  - Resource Exchange
  - Command
  - Control
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchanges Identifier, Resource Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can fill in a Role-based Personnel Connectivity Table by adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges.</span></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Adding existing Resource Exchanges in a Personnel </span>Role-based Connectivity Table</h3><p>To add an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exchange</strong> </span>dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><strong>+</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:</span><br /><ul><li>Resource Exchange</li><li>Command</li><li>Control</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>are added to the table.</li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fa275e3-6096-4d07-a83e-cee5dd4f2328"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource <span>Exchanges </span>Identifier, Resource <span>Exchanges </span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only. </li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722153 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01215: Working with Personnel Sequences diagram

- page_id: `136722153`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722153/Working+with+Personnel+Sequences+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:01.176+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Sequences
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in the Personnel Sequences diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized****Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Sending and Receiving Resources.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource Exchange in the Personnel Sequences diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Resource Exchange</strong></span> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span class="confluence-link"> </span> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Sequences diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Sequences diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and on the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:</span><ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edd9cc73-f3d1-4686-a619-303c55fccc6e"><ac:rich-text-body>Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Sending and Receiving Resources.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchanges data.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722161 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01216: Working with Personnel Structure diagram

- page_id: `136722161`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722161/Working+with+Personnel+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:01.539+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1062887895

1062887898

1062895266

**On this page**

1062887897

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Personnel Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the PersonnelStructure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Personnel Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Personnel Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Organization element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Organization in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Organization and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To create an Organization in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Personnel Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Organizationand select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_organization.png']

Organization

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Organization (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x'].

##### Connecting the Organizations

When you have Organization created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Resource Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Directed Composition** and choose **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='personnel.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='personnel.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x']** dialog, select **Resource****Association.**
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='personnel.str.display_related.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024x'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='personnel.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024x'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='personnel.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

1062887893

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

/></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="220" ac:width="720"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_organization.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7d01530-058c-4324-a12b-1096609019f2"><ac:rich-text-body>The structure of each <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Organization</span> can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Organization</span> (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Organizations</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you have <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Organization</span> created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel_composition_palette.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="420" ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel_structure_draw_composition.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Resource Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="310" ac:width="686"><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel.str.information_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Directed Composition</strong> and choose <strong>Aggregation</strong></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="304" ac:width="349"><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel.str.select_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.</p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="208" ac:width="469"><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel.str.change_role_name.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="677" ac:width="466"><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel.str.set_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><h3>Displaying created structure</h3><p style="text-align: left;">If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p style="text-align: left;"><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Resource </strong><strong>Association.</strong></p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="861" ac:width="686"><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: left;">To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel.str.compartments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel.str.attributes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="656ce213-6e19-4323-893e-cf2fbb992ee5"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p style="text-align: left;">To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="787" ac:width="748"><ri:attachment ri:filename="personnel.str.display_related_nested.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Personnel Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4da1ae2f-3a23-4373-8405-c21f52c94a0c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1062887893</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722178 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01217: Working with Personnel Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `136722178`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722178/Working+with+Personnel+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:02.104+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

486025715

486025718

486025714

**On this page**

486025717

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Personnel Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the PersonnelTaxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**PersonnelTaxonomy**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **PersonnelTaxonomy**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Organization element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Organization (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Personnel Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Organization (or other appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_organization.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Organization (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x'].

##### Connect the elements

When you have Organization (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722187 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01218: Working with Personnel Taxonomy table

- page_id: `136722187`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722187/Working+with+Personnel+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:02.313+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Personnel > Personnel Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Personnel Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button and select Organization, Person, Post, or Responsibility. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Organizational Resource dialog, select the desired Organizational Resources. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div class="wiki-content"><p>To create a Personnel Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select Organization, Person, Post, or Responsibility. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Organizational Resource</strong> dialog, select the desired Organizational Resources. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722697 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01219: Working With Predefined Metric Suites

- page_id: `136722697`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722697/Working+With+Predefined+Metric+Suites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:29.847+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Coverage analysis
- labels: ['create-metric-table', 'show-metric-suites', 'hide-metric-suites', 'calculate-new-metric', 'recalculate', 'add-new-metric-with-different-parameters', 'show-abbreviation-in-column-name']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

450242730

450242732

450242731

In order to start calculating metrics, first you need to create the metric table.

To create a metric table

1. In the Containment tree, select a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working With Predefined Metric Suites' space='UAF12P2024x'] for the metric table.
2. From the shortcut menu, select **Tools** > **Metrics** > **New Metric Table**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The metric suites in the **Create Metric Table** dialog will be listed according to your selected package.
3. Define a table name, change the table owner (if needed), and select one or more desired metric suites.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

The names of the columns can be abbreviated. If you need to change the column names to abbreviations, in the metric table toolbar, click the **Options** button and select the **Show Abbreviation in Column Name**option.

When you have used more than one metric suite, you can hide some metric suites in the table that are not relevant at that time.

To show or hide metric suites in the metric table

1. In the metric table toolbar, click the Show Metric Suites button.
2. Select the metric suites you want to show or hide.

There are three actions with the metrics. You can:

- Calculate New Metric . Select to calculate a new set of metrics using the parameters of the selected rows. Results of the calculation are added to the table.
- Recalculate . Select to recalculate the set of metrics in the selected row with the same parameters.
- Add New Metric with Different Parameters . Select to add a new metric to the table with copied parameters. Then change the parameters and recalculate the metrics.

450242729

*****packagesIn the following table, you will find the information in which packages you can create a metric table for the particular metric suites.

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| Actual Projects Covered by Milestones | ProjectsProjects StructureProjects RoadmapProjects ConnectivityProjects ParametersProjects ProcessesProjects TaxonomyProjects Traceability | Project ViewpointPV-1PV-2 | Acquisition ViewpointAcV-1AcV-2 | NATO Programme ViewpointNPV-1NPV-2 | LogicalLr |
| Actual Projects Covered by Actual Organizational Resources | ProjectsProjects StructureProjects RoadmapProjects ConnectivityProjects ParametersProjects ProcessesProjects TaxonomyProjects Traceability | Project ViewpointPV-1PV-2 | Acquisition ViewpointAcV-1AcV-2 | NATO Programme ViewpointNPV-1NPV-2 | LogicalLr |
| Operational Activities Covered by Functions | OperationalOperational TaxonomyOperational Processes | Operational ViewpointOV-5 | Operational ViewpointOV-5 | NATO Operational ViewpointNOV-5 | LogicalL4 |
| Operational Activities Covered by Operational Performers | OperationalOperational Processes | Operational ViewpointOV-5 | Operational ViewpointOV-5 | NATO Operational ViewpointNOV-5 | LogicalL4 |
| Operational Performers Covered by Resource Performers | OperationalOperational Taxonomy | Operational ViewpointOV-2 | Operational ViewpointOV-2 | NATO Operational ViewpointNOV-2 | LogicalL1 |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722437 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01220: Working with Project Activities to Capabilities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `136722437`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722437/Working+with+Project+Activities+to+Capabilities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:15.556+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282681767

1282681778

1282681777

The Project Activities to Capabilities Mapping (Pj-Tr) shows the mapping between the capabilities and the project activities that those capabilities support directly or using the implied relationship.

The Rows of this matrix are Project Activities and the Columns are Capabilities.

To build the matrix

1. Specify Rows **Scope** (Project Activities).
2. Specify Columns **Scope** ( Capabilities ).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Project Activitiesmap to Capabilities using Maps To Capability relationship.

##### Mapping a Project Activity to a Capability

To map a Project Activity to a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282681766

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723544 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01221: Working with Project Roadmap diagram.mobile.phone

- page_id: `136723544`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723544/Working+with+Project+Roadmap+diagram.mobile.phone
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:35:25.183+02:00`
- ancestors: .brikit.mobile
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

913483310

913483313

913483309

**On this page**

33

913483312

##### Creating Project Roadmap diagram

When you choose to create Project Roadmap diagram, the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Project Roadmap Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a Project Roadmap diagram

1. Right-click the Project Roadmap Package and select Create Diagram > Project Roadmap .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Project Roadmap Creation Wizard** .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Project Roadmap Creation Wizard** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Project Roadmap** check box.

To create a Project Roadmap diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Project Roadmap Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the Project Roadmap Package and select Create Diagram > Project Roadmap .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank Project Roadmap diagram is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

##### Adding Actual Projects

To add a new Actual Project

1. On the Project Roadmap diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . An empty line for the newly created Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.

To add an existing Actual Project

1. On the Project Roadmap diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Actual Project dialog, select an Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. An Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.

##### Adding Actual Sub Projects

To add a new Actual Sub Project

1. Select the Actual Project for which you want to create an Actual Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Project Roadmap diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - Right click Actual Project and select Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. An empty line for the newly created Actual Sub Project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Type its name
5. Specify Start Date and End Date for the Actual Sub Project.

When the Actual Sub Projects are created, the parent Actual Project time frame combines the time frames of all its Actual Sub Projects automatically.

##### Adding Actual Project Milestones

To add an Actual ProjectMilestone in Project Roadmap diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project **Milestone** .
  - right-click a Project and click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

##### Editing Project Status

To edit the Project Status in Project Roadmap diagram

1. On the diagram pane, right-click the milestone and select **Edit Status**. If the milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Edit Status** and then select the proper milestone for which you want to edit the status. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] step 2 opens.
2. Edit the Project Status in the following way: 
a) Select desired project thread in the Project Thread area. 
b) Define selected project thread in the Status Indicator area.
3. Click **Finish** when you are done.

##### Relating Actual Projects

You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.

You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate Actual Projects

1. Select two Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select the relation type:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between Actual Projects

1. Select two related Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect project sequence

1. Right-click the highlighted relation and click Validation .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions. If you change dates of an Actual Project containing Actual Sub Projects, the Actual Sub Project dates change correspondingly.

##### Deleting Actual Project Milestones

To delete an Actual Project Milestone

- In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Delete .

##### Deleting Actual Projects

To delete an Actual Project from the chart

1. Select an Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete an Actual Project from the model

1. Select an Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Removing Project Status

To remove the Project Status

1. In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Edit Status . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] step 2 opens.
2. In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ff31a79-f36a-4c65-b76e-f6d7fc21979e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">913483310</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d678c9e-f347-41ad-8b0e-c2ddf10e8aac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">913483313</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bca94aae-f18f-4e9e-b6e7-9e465695347b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">913483309</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9570a622-473b-421b-a840-36a994074ffc"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba55efbf-3ef0-4be3-829b-75a38572b422"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">913483312</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Creating Project Roadmap diagram</h3><p>When you choose to create Project Roadmap diagram, the<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Project Roadmap Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</p><p>To create a Project Roadmap diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Project Roadmap Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Roadmap</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Roadmap Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c77ed02-f212-4ece-8767-9903e864e6c7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Roadmap Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create Project Roadmap</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a Project Roadmap diagram manually when <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Roadmap Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Project Roadmap Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Roadmap</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press <strong>Enter</strong>. <br />The blank Project Roadmap diagram is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be5825d5-3d47-4481-9536-da3fe0194dee"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the<strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Roadmap Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard</strong> <strong>each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to<em> true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3><span class="toc-item-body"><span class="toc-link">Adding Actual Projects</span></span></h3><p>To add a new Actual Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Project Roadmap diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Project</strong>. An empty line for the newly created Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Actual Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Project Roadmap diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Actual Project</strong> dialog, select an Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />An Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.</li></ol><h3><span class="toc-item-body"><span class="toc-link">Adding Actual Sub Projects</span></span></h3><p>To add a new Actual Sub Project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Actual Project for which you want to create an Actual Sub Project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Project Roadmap diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li><li>Right click Actual Project and select<strong> Add new Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>An empty line for the newly created Actual Sub Project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Type its name</li><li>Specify <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Start Date and End Date </span> for the Actual Sub Project.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9830ca9-eba3-4163-b8c6-1b490abac675"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When the Actual Sub Projects are created, the parent Actual Project time frame combines the time frames of all its Actual Sub Projects automatically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3><span class="toc-item-body"><span class="toc-link">Adding Actual Project Milestones</span></span></h3><p>To add an Actual Project<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>Milestone in Project Roadmap diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong> Actual Project</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Milestone</strong></span>.</li><li>right-click a Project and click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>In Service</li><li>Deployed</li><li>No Longer Used</li><li>Out Of Service</li><li>Other</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<strong> </strong><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button when you are done.</li></ol><h3><span class="toc-item-body"><span class="toc-link">Editing Project Status</span></span></h3><p>To edit the Project Status in Project Roadmap diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the diagram pane, right-click the milestone and select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09bbfbf5-0d29-4034-afb9-5987f3f65b10"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select <strong>Edit Status</strong> and then select the proper milestone for which you want to edit the status.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link> step 2 opens.</p></li><li><p>Edit the Project Status in the following way:<br />a) Select desired project thread in the Project Thread area.<br />b) Define selected project thread in the Status Indicator area.</p></li><li><p class="confluence-link">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p></li></ol><h3><span class="toc-item-body"><span class="toc-link">Relating Actual Projects</span></span></h3><p>You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:</p><ul><li>Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p><br /></p><p>To relate Actual Projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two Actual Projects.</li><li>On the diagram Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation between Actual Projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related Actual Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>None</strong>.<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> <br /></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To resolve the Project Milestones</span></span></h3><p>To delete an Actual Project Milestone</p><hr /><ul><li>In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Status</span></span></h3><p>To remove the Project Status</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select <strong>Edit Status</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link> step 2 opens.</li><li>In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click <ac:image ac:title="Remove project status indicating mark" ac:alt="Remove project status indicating mark"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Project Roadmap diagram.mobile.phone" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 01223: Working with Projects Process Flow diagram

- page_id: `136722390`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722390/Working+with+Projects+Process+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:13.881+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1437829688

1437829699

1437829689

Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] must be created first.

To create a Project Process Flow diagram for Project Activity flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Organizational Resource or Project Role to be represented by the Swimlane. If you click Cancel in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Organizational Resource or Project Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Project Activity Actions or drop Project Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Project Activity Actions wit h Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Projects Process Flow diagram with the top elementas a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ProjectsProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Projects Activity Action. You can create an inner Projects Process Flow diagram for a Projects Activity Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create a Projects Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Projects Activity Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Projects Activity Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Projects Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Organizational Resource or Project Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new Projects Process Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Project Activity Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Organizational Resource or Resource Role and Project Activity (the behavior of the Project Activity Action).

1437829687

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MD2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722389 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01224: Working with Projects Processes diagram

- page_id: `136722389`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722389/Working+with+Projects+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:13.811+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Project Processes diagram for Project Activity hierarchies

1. Create Project Activities.
2. Create Resource Performers or reuse them from Project Taxonomy view (recommended).
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Resource Performers and Project Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Project Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Project Processes diagram for Project Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Project Activities.</li><li>Create Resource Performers or reuse them from Project Taxonomy view (recommended).</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Resource Performers and Project Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Project Activities if necessary.</li></ol>
````
