## PAGE 01225: Working with Projects Roadmap diagram

- page_id: `136722395`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722395/Working+with+Projects+Roadmap+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:14.096+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

913483310

913483313

913483309

**On this page**

33

913483312

##### Creating Projects Roadmap diagram

When you choose to create Projects Roadmap diagram, the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Projects Roadmap Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a Projects Roadmap diagram

1. Right-click the Projects Roadmap Package and select Create Diagram > Projects Roadmap .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Projects Roadmap Creation Wizard** .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Projects Roadmap Creation Wizard** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Projects Roadmap** check box.

To create a Projects Roadmap diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Projects Roadmap Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the Projects Roadmap Package and select Create Diagram > Projects Roadmap .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank Projects Roadmap diagram is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog, select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

##### Adding Actual Projects

To add a new Actual Project

1. On the Projects Roadmap diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . An empty line for the newly created Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the End Date*.

***** The**End Date** will be empty by default. The **Start Date** will be set automatically depending on whether you are working with an existing or new roadmap diagram. If you are working on the new diagram, the current date will be set as the start date. If you are working on the existing diagram, the start date will be set to correspond with the dates of the earlier created Actual Projects. You can change the start date in the Actual Project **Specification** window.

To add an existing Actual Project

1. On the Projects Roadmap diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Actual Project dialog, select an Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. An Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.

##### Adding Actual Sub Projects

To add a new Actual Sub Project

1. Select the Actual Project for which you want to create an Actual Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Projects Roadmap diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - Right-click Actual Project and select Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. An empty line for the newly created Actual Sub Project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Specify the End Date*.

***** The**End Date** will be empty by default. The **Start Date** will be set automatically depending on whether you are working with an existing or new roadmap diagram. If you are working on the new diagram, the current date will be set as the start date. If you are working on the existing diagram, the start date will be set to correspond with the dates of the earlier created Actual Sub Projects. You can change the start date in the Actual Sub Project **Specification** window.

When the Actual Sub Projects are created, the parent Actual Project time frame combines the time frames of all its Actual Sub Projects automatically.

##### Adding Actual Project Milestones

To add an Actual ProjectMilestone in Projects Roadmap diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project **Milestone** .
  - Right-click a Project and click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

##### Editing Project Status

To edit the Project Status in Projects Roadmap diagram

1. On the diagram pane, right-click the milestone and select **Edit Status**. If the milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu, select **Edit Status** and then select the proper milestone for which you want to edit the status. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] step 2 opens.
2. Edit the Project Status in the following way: 
a) Select desired project thread in the Project Thread area. 
b) Define the selected project thread in the Status Indicator area.
3. Click **Finish** when you are done.

##### Relating Actual Projects

You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.

You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate Actual Projects

1. Select two Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select the relation type:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between Actual Projects

1. Select two related Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect project sequence

1. Right-click the highlighted relation and click Validation .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions. If you change the dates of an Actual Project containing Actual Sub Projects, the Actual Sub Project dates change correspondingly.

##### Deleting Actual Project Milestones

To delete an Actual Project Milestone

- In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Delete .

##### Deleting Actual Projects

To delete an Actual Project from the chart

1. Select an Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete an Actual Project from the model

1. Select an Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Removing Project Status

To remove the Project Status

1. In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Edit Status . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] step 2 opens.
2. In the bottom right corner of the Preview area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

725558147

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering table data' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722401 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01226: Working with Projects Structure diagram

- page_id: `136722401`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722401/Working+with+Projects+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:14.364+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1147916981

1147916985

1147916980

**On this page**

1147916984

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Projects Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Projects Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Projects Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Projects Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Project element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Project in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Project and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To create an Project in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Projects Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Project and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_project.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Project (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x'].

##### Connecting the Projects

When you have Project created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Project Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Directed Aggregation** and choose **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='projects.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='projects.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x']** dialog, select **Association**.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='projects.str.display_related.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024x'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='projects_compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024x'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='projects.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Projects Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

1147925220

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MD2024x']

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ccad12d4-888c-4abe-8f8f-605cd14357b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1147916981</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd0ed3a7-fbc4-49e4-adf7-9472e0fc9b27"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1147916985</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="516fec38-3d7e-4861-a5ba-1a6e6f44fb75"><ac:parameter ac:macro-id="debf5bd7-080d-4392-b7ba-aac5be05b84e" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8699734f-117a-41d3-98d6-325d3242ff25"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1147916984</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Projects Structure diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the Projects Structure and do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Projects Structure</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="210" ac:width="318"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_projects_structure_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Projects Structure </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Project element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Project in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Project and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="243" ac:width="510"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_projects_project.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Project in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right click the Projects Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Project and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="498" ac:width="353"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_projects_project_containment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="217" ac:width="707"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_project.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Project (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the Projects</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you have Project created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="projects_composition_pallet.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="232" ac:width="742"><ri:attachment ri:filename="projects_draw_composition.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Project Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="295" ac:width="398"><ri:attachment ri:filename="projects.str.information_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Directed Aggregation</strong> and choose <strong>Aggregation</strong></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="345" ac:width="348"><ri:attachment ri:filename="projects_select_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="206" ac:width="330"><ri:attachment ri:filename="projects.str.change_role_name.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="681" ac:width="328"><ri:attachment ri:filename="projects.str.set_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><h3>Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association</strong>.</p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="830" ac:width="613"><ri:attachment ri:filename="projects.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="projects_compartments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e8b7196-5d28-4a00-bd6e-b2bd3173848e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li style="text-align: left;">Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="752" ac:width="714"><ri:attachment ri:filename="projects.str.display_related_nested.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fbedaf7-ac4d-4963-a238-59b603e1a441"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1147925220</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722418 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01227: Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `136722418`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722418/Working+with+Projects+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:14.825+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

827966462

827966464

827966461

**On this page**

827966463

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Project Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Project Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Project Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_projects_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Project Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Project element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Project (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_project.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Project Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Project (or other appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_project.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Project (or other appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Project (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Make sure that <strong>All</strong> is chosen for <strong>Properties</strong>.</li><li>Search for <strong>Is Abstract</strong> and set it ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.</li></ol><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Projects Taxonomy diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722427 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01228: Working with Projects Taxonomy table

- page_id: `136722427`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722427/Working+with+Projects+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:15.078+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Projects > Projects Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Projects Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Project dialog, select the desired projects. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Projects Taxonomy table</p><hr /><div class="wiki-content"><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Project</strong> dialog, select the desired projects. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722198 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01229: Working with Resources Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `136722198`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722198/Working+with+Resources+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:02.894+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating Resources Connectivity diagram

The Resources Connectivity diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] should be completed.

To create a Resources Connectivity diagram

1. Create resources.
2. Connect resources using Association.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Associations.

The Resources Connectivity product also allows you to show the Capabilities. You can model internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']Resources Structure diagram.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Processes' space='UAF12P2024x'] as soon as the Resources Connectivity has been completed.

##### Creating Resource Exchanges in Resources Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchange in Resources Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect Organizational Resources.
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Physical Resources.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722209 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01230: Working with Resources Connectivity matrix

- page_id: `136722209`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722209/Working+with+Resources+Connectivity+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:03.426+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

914417970

914417972

914417969

**On this page**

914417971

A Resources Connectivity matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource Exchangesand their headers represent Resources. Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource Exchangebetween resources. A Resources Connectivity matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

##### Creating Resource Connectivity matrix

To create a Resources Connectivity matrix

1. Specify the **Row Scope** (Resources).
2. Specify the **Column Scope** (Resources).
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Resources Connectivity matrix

To add a new Resource Exchange

1. Right-click the intersection in the matrix and select Add New Resource Exchange .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard .
3. Click Finish when you are done. A new Resource Exchange appears in the selected intersection.

To remove an existing Resource Exchange

1. Right-click the filled intersection.
2. Under the Delete section, choose the Resource Exchange you want to remove from the model.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722207 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01231: Working with Resources Connectivity Table

- page_id: `136722207`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722207/Working+with+Resources+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:03.223+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Resources Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add new or existing Resource Exchange to fill in an Resources Connectivity Table.

##### Adding new Resource Exchanges

To add new Resource Exchange

1. In the Resources Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges

To add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the Resources Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Exchange dialog opens.
2. Click + button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:
  - Resource Exchange
  - Command
  - Control
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Resources Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource Exchange to fill in an Resources Connectivity Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Resources Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><strong>+</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:</span><br /><ul><li>Resource Exchange</li><li>Command</li><li>Control</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.</span></span> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722213 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01232: Working with Resources Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `136722213`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722213/Working+with+Resources+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:03.679+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating Resources Constraints Definition diagram

The Resources Constraints Definition diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] should be completed.

To create a Resources Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create resources.
2. Connect resources using Association.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Associations.

The Resource Taxonomy product also allows you to show the Capabilities. You can model the internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Processes' space='UAF12P2024x'] as soon as the Resources Taxonomy has been completed.

##### Creating Resource Exchanges in Resources Constraints Definition diagram

To create a Resource Exchange in Resources Constraints Definition diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect the Organizational Resources
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Physical Resources.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Resource Information Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Creating Resources Constraints Definition diagram</h3><p>The Resources Constraints Definition diagram requires that the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span> should be completed.</p><p>To create a Resources Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create resources.</li><li>Connect resources using Association.</li><li>Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Associations.</li></ol><p>The Resource Taxonomy product also allows you to show the Capabilities. You can model the internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</p><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resources Processes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources Processes view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> as soon as the Resources Taxonomy has been completed.</p><h3>Creating Resource Exchanges in Resources Constraints Definition diagram</h3><p>To create a Resource Exchange in Resources Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Constraints Definition diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Constraints Definition diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and on the diagram pane, connect resources.</li><li>Click the Control relationship and connect the Organizational Resources</li><li>Click the Command relationship and connect Physical Resources.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:</span><ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2f47c85-d2d3-41e4-8e6a-68dc9af369e2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only Resource Information Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link">You can use <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchange Manager</span> </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722216 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01233: Working with Resources Constraints table

- page_id: `136722216`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722216/Working+with+Resources+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:03.843+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resources Constraints table by adding a new Resource

1. Click the Add New button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is added.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the cell and in the Select Subject Of Resource Constraint dialog, choose Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization , Post, Function, Resource Information, or Resource Exchange.
3. Define the **Rule Specification**. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.
4. Select the Rule Kind from one of the following:
  - Structural Assertion
  - Action Assertion
  - Derivation
  - Contract
  - Constraint
  - Guidance
  - Security Policy
  - Caveat

You can assign more than one Resource for one constraint.

To create a Resources Constraints table by adding and existing Resource

- Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resources Constraints table by adding a new Resource</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add New </strong>button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is added.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Constraints table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> in the cell and in the <strong>Select Subject Of Resource Constraint</strong> dialog, choose <span>Resource Artifact,</span> Software, Capability Configuration, Organization<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, Post, Function, Resource Information, or Resource Exchange</span>. </span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Define the <strong>Rule Specification</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d7218cc-0982-4a1e-a68c-e9dde5ebd074"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Select the <strong>Rule Kind</strong> from one of the following:<ul><li>Structural Assertion</li><li>Action Assertion</li><li>Derivation</li><li>Contract</li><li>Constraint</li><li>Guidance</li><li>Security Policy</li><li>Caveat</li></ul></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f10bb7bd-1c36-41bc-a773-c181b23d6555"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can assign more than one Resource for one constraint.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create a Resources Constraints table by adding and existing Resource</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.</li></ul>
````

## PAGE 01234: Working with Resources Evolution diagram

- page_id: `136722240`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating Resources Evolution diagram

To create a Resources Evolution diagram

1. .
2. .
3. .

###### newCreate new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in Resources Evolution diagram

1. Click the Add New button and select Version of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

###### defineDefine the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Do one of the following:
  - Drag the resource onto the Version of Configuration.
  - Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select Resource .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Assignment Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

The result of this procedure is in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Removing Resources from a Resources Evolution diagram

To remove a Resource

1. Right-click the Resource bar and from the shortcut menu, select Remove Resource > <name of the resource you want to remove>.

##### Removing Version of Configurations from a Resources Evolution diagram

To delete a Version of Configuration

1. Select a Version of Configuration.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722244 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01235: Working with Resources Forecast table

- page_id: `136722244`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722244/Working+with+Resources+Forecast+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:05.978+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1608782209

1608782211

1608782210

To create a Resources Forecast table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1608782206

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722201 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01237: Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `136722201`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722201/Working+with+Resources+Internal+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:03.034+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

736502083

736502085

994228161

**On this page**

736502084

##### Creating Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

The Resources Internal Connectivity diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']should be completed.

To create a Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchangein Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click the Resource Connector.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Resource Information can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables you to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

##### Showing or hiding realized Resource Exchanges

You can choose to either show or hide the Resource Exchange Item on the element realizing Resource Exchanges.

To show/hide the realized Resource Exchange Items

1. On the diagram, click the element for which you want to show or hide the realized exchange.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space='MD2024x'] :
  1. If theResource Exchange Item is not displayed , the tooltip for the button is [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_show_hide.png'] Show Realized Resource Exchanges . Click it to hide the Resource ExchangeItem.
  2. If the Resource ExchangeItem is displayed , the tooltip for the button is[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Hide Realized Resource **Exchanges** . Click it to hide the Resource ExchangeItem. 
If theelement realizes only one Resource Exchange, the Resource ExchangeItem is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.
3. If the element realizes multiple Resource Exchanges, the Resource Exchange Items dialog opens. In the dialog, do the following:
  1. Select the check-boxes for the Resource Exchanges whose Resource Exchange Item you want to be displayed.
  2. Clear the check-boxes for the Resource Exchanges whose Resource ExchangeItem you want to be hidden. To select all check boxes, click the**Select All**button. To clear all check-boxes, click the**Clear All**button.
  3. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='show_resource_exchanges.png']

736502082

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Learn more**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space='UAF12P2024x'].** You cancheck the consistency of the Resources Internal Connectivity diagram with Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Resources Internal Connectivity diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

manipulator toolbar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:<ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the<span> <span style="color: ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_show_hide.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong>Show Realized Resource Exchanges</strong>. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click it to hide the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Resource Exchange </span>Item.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Resource Exchange </span>Item</span> is displayed<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, the tooltip for the button is<span> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_hide.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Hide Realized Resource <strong>Exchanges</strong></strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Click it to hide the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Resource Exchange </span>Item.<br />If the<span> </span>element realizes only one Resource Exchange, the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Resource Exchange </span>Item is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.<br /></span></li></ol></li><li>If the element realizes multiple Resource Exchanges, the Resource Exchange Items dialog opens. In the dialog, do the following:<ol><li>Select the check-boxes for the Resource Exchanges whose <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Resource Exchange </span>Item you want to be displayed.</li><li><p>Clear the check-boxes for the Resource Exchanges whose <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Resource Exchange </span>Item you want to be hidden.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3fe22c9d-e07f-4265-afb2-8ceeecce5e43"><ac:rich-text-body>To select all check boxes, click the<span> </span><strong>Select All<span> </span></strong>button. To clear all check-boxes, click the<span> </span><strong>Clear All<span> </span></strong>button.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click<span> </span><strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_resource_exchanges.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55ebed67-e480-4eed-8e2c-52c585419c6a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">736502082</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Resource Role Kind mapping table by OMG specification"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Role" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resource Role Kind mapping table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Learn more</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="UAF Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ac:link>.</strong> You can</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> check the consistency of the Resources Internal Connectivity diagram with Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Resources Internal Connectivity diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722225 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01238: Working with Resources Parametric diagram

- page_id: `136722225`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722225/Working+with+Resources+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:04.387+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Parameters
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

496491094

496491096

496491095

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024x'] to constrain the properties of another Resource Performer (e.g. Capability Configuration, Resource Artifact, System). It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024x'] and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Resource Role or Measurements) from within that internal Resource Performer context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

496491093

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Resource Role or Measurements) from within that internal Resource Performer context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95bf8296-5b2c-47b6-842b-3e7eaa6ca7d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496491093</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722229 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01239: Working with Resources Processes diagram

- page_id: `136722229`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722229/Working+with+Resources+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:04.794+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the Resources Processes diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Processes' space='UAF12P2024x']Operational Processes and Resources Structure diagrams should be created first.

To create a Resources Processes diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) resources from Resource Structure, Resource Taxonomy diagrams.
3. Use Is Capable To Perform r elationship to connect the resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Processes' space='UAF12P2024x']Operational Processes diagrams.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f52e3b83-f45c-4cd2-ac62-09f325eff963"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the Resources Processes diagram, the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Processes" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Operational Processes</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> and <span class="confluence-link">Resources Structure</span> diagrams should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a Resources Processes diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) resources from<span class="confluence-link"> Resource Structure, Resource Taxonomy diagrams.</span></li><li>Use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Is Capable To Perform r</span>elationship to connect the resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Processes" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Operational Processes</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> diagrams.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722230 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01240: Working with Resources Processes Flow diagram

- page_id: `136722230`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722230/Working+with+Resources+Processes+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:04.944+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1428128584

1428128587

1428128586

Before creating the Resources Processes Flow diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']Resources Processes diagram should be created first.

##### Creating Resources Processes Flow diagram

To create a Resource Process Flow diagram for Function Action flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Resource Performer or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click **Cancel**in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Resource Performer or Resource Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Function Action or drop them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Function Action wit h Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Resource Process Flow diagram with the top element -*MRT Searcher*as a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ResourceProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Function Action or Resource Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Resource Process Flow diagram for a Function Action or Resource Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create a ResourceProcess Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Function Action or Resource Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Function Action or Resource Action.
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Resource Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Resource Performer or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new ResourceProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Function Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Resource Performer and Function (the behavior of the Function Action).

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Resources Processes Flow diagram

To create a Resource Exchangein the Resources Processes Flow diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and then click Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables you to analyze and manage the ResourceExchangesdata.

1428128583

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MD2024x']
