<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722208 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01241: Working with Resources Role-based Connectivity Table

- page_id: `136722208`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722208/Working+with+Resources+Role-based+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:03.315+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can fill in a Resources Role-based Connectivity Table by adding new or existing Resource Exchanges.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges in a ResourcesRole-based Connectivity Table

There should be the realized resource exchanges between Resource Roles in your model.

To add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource **Exchange** dialog opens.
2. Click + button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:
  - Resource Exchange
  - Command
  - Control
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchanges Identifier, Resource Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can fill in a Resources Role-based Connectivity Table by adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges.</span></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Adding existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges in a Resources </span></span>Role-based Connectivity Table</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17e00dca-729a-4383-b9de-8a724ec104c1"><ac:rich-text-body>There should be the realized resource exchanges between Resource Roles in your model.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exchange</strong> </span>dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><strong>+</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:</span><br /><ul><li>Resource Exchange</li><li>Command</li><li>Control</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>Identifier, Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.<span> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722249 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01242: Working with Resources Sequences diagram

- page_id: `136722249`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722249/Working+with+Resources+Sequences+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:06.372+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Sequences
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in the Resources Sequences diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized****Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Sending and Receiving Resources.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource Exchange in the Resources Sequences diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Resource Exchange</strong></span> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span class="confluence-link"> </span> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Sequences diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Sequences diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and on the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector.</li></ul></li><li>Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:<ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edd9cc73-f3d1-4686-a619-303c55fccc6e"><ac:rich-text-body>Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Sending and Receiving Resources.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchanges data.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722258 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01243: Working with Resources Structure diagram

- page_id: `136722258`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722258/Working+with+Resources+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:07.141+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

565633333

565633336

565633332

**On this page**

565633334

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Resource Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the ResourceStructure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**ResourceStructure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **ResourceStructure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Resource Artifact element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Resource Artifact in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Resource Artifact and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To create an Resource Artifact in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Resource Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Resource Artifact and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_resource_artifact.png']

Resource Artifact

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Resource Artifact (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x'].

##### Connecting the Resource Artifact

When you have Resource Artifact created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Resource Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK .

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024x'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024x'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

565641494

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abe3734a-44b5-4d45-8a04-40ea23304a35"><ac:rich-text-body>If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the <strong>Resource Association</strong> also.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.</li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="891" ac:width="740"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.compartments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="879f1986-4eb4-464d-879b-270ccfa1b21e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.attributes.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="722" ac:width="756"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.display_related_nested.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42578d68-761f-4ee6-a194-b85848d06184"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">565641494</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722276 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01244: Working with Resources Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `136722276`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722276/Working+with+Resources+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:07.784+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

547864447

547864449

547864446

**On this page**

547864448

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Resources Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Resources Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Resources Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_resources_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Resources Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Capability Configuration element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Capability Configuration (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_cap_config.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Resources Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Capability Configuration (or other appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_cap_config.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability Configuration (or other appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Capability Configuration(or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722287 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01245: Working with Resources Taxonomy table

- page_id: `136722287`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722287/Working+with+Resources+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:08.210+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resources Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button and select Capability Configuration, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Organization, Person, Post, Resource Architecture, Resource Artifact, Resource Mitigation, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, System, or Technology. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Resource Performer dialog, select the desired Resources. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resources Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select Capability Configuration, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Organization, Person, Post, Resource Architecture, Resource Artifact, Resource Mitigation, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, System, or Technology. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Resource Performer</strong> dialog, select the desired Resources. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722295 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01246: Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `136722295`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722295/Working+with+Resources+to+Capabilities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:08.959+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282802627

1282802639

1282802629

**Resources to Capabilities Mapping (Rs-Tr)** addresses the direct and indirect linkage between resources (Operational Agent, Resource Performer, Service, Actual Service, Actual Resource) and Capabilities specified in the Strategy domain.

The Rows of this matrix are resources (Operational Agent, Resource Performer, Service, Actual Service, Actual Resource) and the Columns are Capabilities.

To build the Matrix

1. Specify Rows scope (Resources);
2. Specify Columns scope (Strategy);
3. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.

Resources map to capabilities using the "Exhibits" relationship.

To map a resource to a Capability, double-click the intersection between the desired elements. Double-clicking the intersection again will delete the relationship.

1282802626

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Double-clicking the intersection again will delete the relationship.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="820b48fa-6ed9-46d8-acda-7b2dc00bb987"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282802626</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with matrices" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722293 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01247: Working with Resources to Operational Activities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `136722293`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722293/Working+with+Resources+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:08.792+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources > Resources Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282791216

1282791227

1282791226

**The Resources to Operational Activities Mapping (Rs-Tr)**describesmapping between the Systems and Operational Activities.

The Rows of this matrix are Resources and the Columns are Operational Activities.

To build the matrix

1. Specify Rows **Scope** (Resources);
2. Specify Columns **Scope** (Operational Activities);
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.

Resources map to Operational Activities through transitive relationship. It consists of mapping between Operational Activities and Functions using Implements relationship and mapping between Resources and Functions using Is Capable To Perform relationship.

1282791215

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024x']

It consists of mapping between Operational Activities and Functions using Implements relationship and mapping between Resources and Functions using Is Capable To Perform relationship. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db6b9137-2a9a-4d73-be5f-3d6d41e769ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282791215</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with matrices" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722497 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01248: Working with Risks diagram

- page_id: `136722497`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722497/Working+with+Risks+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:18.465+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Parameters Pm
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1342409104

1342409115

1341185000

**On this page**

1342409105

The **Risk**diagram shows the relevant risks along with associated measures like the likelihood of occurrence and potential negative consequences.

The Risk diagram can be created under the Parameter package of each domain (e.g Operational Parameters, Strategic Parameters)

##### Creating a diagram

An example is shown with the Operational Parameters, but the Risk diagram can be created under other domain Parameters packages also.

To create a Risk diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Parameters and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Risk**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Risk**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Risk element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create a Risk in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Risk and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Risk in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the package in the Security domain, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Risk and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_risk.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or another appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x'].

##### Connect the Risk

When you have the Risk created, you can start connecting them using the relationships, such as Affects, Affects In Context, and Evoked By.

To connect the elements with the Affects In Context relationship

1. Select an element from which the Affects In Context will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Affects In Context. You can also select the Affects In Context relationship from the diagram palette.
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1342409094

## PAGE 01249: Working with Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `136722376`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722376/Working+with+Risks+to+Assets+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:13.175+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282882341

1282882352

1282882342

The Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix****(Sc-Tr) shows which risks are applicable to Asset Roles. The Rowsof this matrix are Risks and the Columns are Assets.

To build the matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Risks);
2. Specify the Column Scope (Assets);
3. Click the Refresh button.

To map a Risks to an Assets

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Affects.

To remove the relation from the matrix

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282882340

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721633 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01250: Working with S1 Service Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `136721633`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721633/Working+with+S1+Service+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:27.294+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Service Taxonomy Table (S1)**provides all the available service types.

To add a new Service or Service Architecture in the Service Taxonomy table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button and choose Service or Service Architecture. A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Service or Service Architecture.

To add an existing Service or Service Architecture in the Service Taxonomy table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Service dialog, choose the desired Services or Service Architectures.
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Services or Service Architectures.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<span> </span><strong>Service Taxonomy Table (S1)</strong><span> </span>provides all the available service types.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new Service or Service Architecture in the Service Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Add New</strong><span> </span>button and choose Service or Service Architecture. A new row is added.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Service or Service Architecture.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Service or Service Architecture in the Service Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong><span> </span>button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Service</strong> dialog, choose the desired Services or Service Architectures.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Services or Service Architectures.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721631 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01251: Working with S1 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `136721631`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721631/Working+with+S1+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:27.166+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **S1****Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a S1 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>S1 </strong><strong>Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a S1 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with S1 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722303 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01252: Working with Security Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `136722303`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722303/Working+with+Security+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:09.592+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating Resources Connectivity diagram

The Security Connectivity diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] should be completed.

To create a Security Connectivity diagram

1. Create resources.
2. Connect resources using Association.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Associations.

The Security Connectivity product also allows you to show the Capabilities. You can model internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the Security Structure diagram[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Security Processes' space='UAF12P2024x'] as soon as the Resources Connectivity has been completed.

##### Creating Resource Exchanges in Security Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchange in Security Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect Organizational Resources.
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Physical Resources.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722316 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01253: Working with Security Connectivity Table

- page_id: `136722316`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722316/Working+with+Security+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:10.347+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Security Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Operational Performers. You can add new or existing Operational / Resource Exchange to fill in an Security Connectivity Table.

##### Adding new Operational Exchanges

To add new Operational / Resource Exchange

1. In the Security Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Operational Exchanges

To add an existing Operational / Resource Exchange

1. In the Security Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational / Resource Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operationa / Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational / Resource Exchange Identifier, Operational / Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Operational Performer, Receiving Operational Performer, and Implements cells are read only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An  Security Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Operational Performers. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational / Resource Exchange to fill in an Security Connectivity Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Operational / Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Security Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Operational / Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the  Security Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational / Resource Exchanges by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operationa / Resource Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational / Resource Exchange Identifier, Operational / Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li><span>Sending Operational Performer, Receiving <span>Operational Performer</span>, and Implements cells are read only.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use</span> <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span></span><span> </span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use</span> <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.</span></span><span> </span><br /></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722320 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01254: Working with Security Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `136722320`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722320/Working+with+Security+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:10.905+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1163362754

1163362771

1163362753

**On this page**

1163362755

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Security Constraints Definition diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Security Constraints package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Security Constraints Definition . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_security_constraints_definition.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Security Constraints Definition and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements (assets). An example is described using the Security Enclave element, but the same is valid for other assets.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_security_enclave.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the Security Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).

##### Creating Resource Exchanges in Security Constraints Definition diagram

To create a Resource Exchange

1. Open the **Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the asset (e.g. Security Enclave) and from its smart manipulator toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then click another asset to connect them.
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange_palette.png'] and on the diagram palette, and connect the assets.
2. Specify the Exchange Name, Resource Exchange Type.
3. Select the Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Geo Political Extent Exchange
  - Resource Communication
  - Resource Energy Flow
  - Resource Movement
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Creating and applying Security Constraints

You can create the Security Constraints in the same way as other elements - from the diagram palette or in the Containment tree. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']. Also, you can create constraints in the selected asset's Specification window.

To create a Security Constraint in the Specification window

1. Select a needed asset and open its Specification window.
2. In the Constraints property group, click Create > Security > Security Constraint .
3. Specify the name.
4. In the Specification cell, define the rule. The specification(rule) can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

There are the following constrained elements that can be a subject of the Security Constraint:

1. Asset
2. Asset Role
3. Exchange
4. Security Process

To apply Security Constraints, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, select a Security Constraint and drag it on the element.
- In the needed element's Specification window, go to Constraints property group. Click the Apply button and from the Select Element window, choose a constraint.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722328 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01255: Working with Security Constraints table

- page_id: `136722328`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722328/Working+with+Security+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:11.238+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Security Constraints** table identifies the constraints that are imposed on systems functionality due to some aspects of system design or implementation.

To create a Security Constraints table

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Select Subject Of Security Constraint** dialog, choose the needed constrained element(s):
  - Asset
  - Asset Role
  - Exchange
  - Security Process
3. Click **OK**.
4. In the**Rule****Specification** cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify the body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

To add an existing Security Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, select Security Constraints. The filled row is added to the table.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Security Constraints table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Security Constraints</strong> table identifies the constraints that are imposed on systems functionality due to some aspects of system design or implementation.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Security Constraints table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add New </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">button. The new row is added.</span></li><li>In the<strong> Applies To</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Security Constraints table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>and  in the <strong>Select Subject Of Security Constraint</strong> dialog, choose the needed constrained element(s):</span><br /><ul><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Asset</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Asset Role</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Exchange</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Security Process</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the<strong> Rule </strong><strong>Specification</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Security Constraints table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>and in</span> the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, select the language and specify the body.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd8254de-6c02-4462-a6df-36afcf72fabc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></p><p>To add an existing Security Constraint</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. </li><li>In the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, select Security Constraints. The filled row is added to the table. </li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the Security Constraints table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722378 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01256: Working with Security Controls diagram

- page_id: `136722378`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722378/Working+with+Security+Controls+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:13.317+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Motivation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Security Controls diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Security Motivation and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Security Controls . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_security_controls.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Security Controls and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. To illustrate the example, Security Control and Risk elements are used, but you can also choose Enhances Security Control, Security Control Family, or Enhances as well as Security Risk.

To create a Security Control in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click Security Control and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.
2. Name the element.

To create a Security Control in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Security Motivation package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Select Security Control .
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability.

##### Connect the elements

When you have Security Control created, you can start connecting them using the Mitigates relationship.

To connect the elements with the Mitigates relationship

1. Select an element from which the Mitigates will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Mitigates. You can also select the Mitigates relationship from the diagram palette.
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create an Security Controls diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select the Security Motivation and do one of the following:<ul><li> From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Security Controls</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="367" ac:width="329"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_security_controls.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Security Controls diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Security Controls </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. To illustrate the example, Security Control and Risk elements are used, but you can also choose  Enhances Security Control, Security Control Family, or Enhances as well as Security Risk.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Security Control in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click Security Control and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.<br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Security Control in the Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Right click the Security Motivation package and from the shortcut menu, select </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722375 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 01257: Working with Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `136722375`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722375/Working+with+Security+Controls+to+Risks+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:13.114+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282871885

1282871887

1282871886

The Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix****(Sc-Tr) shows which operational or resource asset roles mitigate risks. The Rows of this matrix are Security Controls and the Columns are Risks.

To build the matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Security Controls);
2. Specify the Column Scope (Risks);
3. Click the Refresh button.

To map an Security Controls to a Risk

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Mitigate.

To remove the relation from the matrix

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282871884

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ab5973f-b447-44b3-93ae-3c7364f19871"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282871885</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="46eac271-5942-433f-936a-15d4b8f059a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282871887</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ce2c90b-5c0d-437d-97ed-1a23f7599ffa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282871886</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix<strong> </strong>(Sc-Tr) shows which operational or resource asset roles mitigate risks. The Rows of this matrix are Security Controls and the Columns are Risks.</p><p> </p><p>To build the matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Security Controls);</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Risks);</li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button.</li></ol><p>     </p><p>To map an Security Controls to a Risk</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.</li><li>Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Mitigate.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation from the matrix</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60f57012-0c0d-489c-9529-e2d5d8930201"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282871884</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with matrices" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 01258: Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

- page_id: `136722306`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722306/Working+with+Security+Internal+Connectivity+Operational+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:09.788+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1912487812

1912487823

1912487813

##### Creating a Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

The Resource Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] should be completed.

To create a Resource Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

1. Create Operational Roles and Operational Ports.
2. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors.

##### Creating Operational Exchange in Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='security_create_op_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='security_operational_exchange.png'] and then click the Operational Connector.
2. Select the Operational Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Configuration
  - Energy
  - Geo Political Extent
  - Information
  - Materiel
  - Organizational
3. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Operational Exchangedata.

1912487811

**Learn more**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space='UAF12P2024x'].** You cancheck the consistency of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram with Operational Activities, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 01259: Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram

- page_id: `136722312`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722312/Working+with+Security+Internal+Connectivity+Resource+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:10.084+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1912563278

1912563298

1912563288

##### Creating a Security Internal Connectivity diagram

The Resource Structure diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] should be completed.

To create a Resource Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Security Internal Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchange in the Security Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='security_resource_exchange.png'] and then click the Resource Connector.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Resource Information can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

1912563277

**Learn more**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space='UAF12P2024x'].** You cancheck the consistency of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram with Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 01260: Working with Security Parametric. Operational diagram

- page_id: `136722331`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722331/Working+with+Security+Parametric.+Operational+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:11.575+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Parameters
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

496649791

496649793

496649792

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024x'] to constrain the properties of another Operational Mitigation. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024x'] and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Operational Role or Measurements) from within that internal Operational Mitigation context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

496649790

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722338 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01262: Working with Security Process Flow (Operational) diagram

- page_id: `136722338`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722338/Working+with+Security+Process+Flow+Operational+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:12.177+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Security > Security Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1430382840

INLINE

1430382852

INLINE

1430382842

INLINE

A Security Process Flow diagram represents flows of security processes. Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] must be created first.

The owner of the Security Process Flow diagram is always a Security Process.

To create a Security Process Flow diagram for Security Process Action flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Agent or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click Cancel in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Operational Agent or Operational Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Security Process Actions/Operational Activity Actions or drop Security Process/Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Security Process Actions/Operational Activity Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the operational structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Security Process Flow diagram with the top elementas a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SecurityProcess Flow diagram is created. You can add either Security Process Action or Operational Action in the Swimlane. You can create an inner Security Process Flow diagram for a Security Process Action or Operational Action Smart Manipulator toolbar in both cases.

To create a SecurityProcess Flow diagram from a Smart Manipulator of a Security Process Action or Operational Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Security Process Action or Operational Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Security Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Agent or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have an operational structure defined.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new SecurityProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Security Process Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Operational Agent and the Security Process (the behavior of the Security Process Action). Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Security Process Action.

1430382839

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MD2024x']

1430382840

1430382852

1430382842

A Security Process Flow diagram represents flows of security processes. Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Processes diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] must be created first.

The owner of Security Process Flow diagram is always a Security Process.

To create a Security Process Flow diagram for Security Process Action flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Resource Performer or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click **Cancel**in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Resource Performer or Resource Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Security Process Actions/Function Actions or drop Security Process/Function from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Security Process Actions/Function Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Security Process Flow diagram with the top elementas a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SecurityProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Security Process Action or Function Action. In both cases, you can create an inner SecurityProcess Flow diagram for a Security Process Action or Function Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create a SecurityProcess Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Security Process Action or Function Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Security Process Action or Function Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Security Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Resource Performer or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new SecurityProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Security Process Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Resource Performer and Security Process (the behavior of the Security Process Action). Furthermore, if the Resource Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Security Process Action.
