ac:macro-id="dcaf8c6b-a839-4ebc-a4b9-75d2529195f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rows</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b0cd39-cf76-49f2-a56a-f7e14c54e52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">columns</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Capabilities as columns in the table</span></h4><p>To add Capabilities as table columns</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add/ Remove Columns </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Capabilities</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb2dee6a-f048-4967-9303-5566fa427b05"><ac:rich-text-body><div class="confluence-information-macro-body"><ul><li>An<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f315cbe8-b530-4eda-806c-177157d1eeee">Exhibits</ac:inline-comment-marker> </span>relationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.</li></ul></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d323a01-8c43-4fb4-ab92-2179fc127d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">cells</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>, select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Removing Capabilities from a Strategic Actual Deployment table</h3><p>To <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0f317402-cd35-47da-9fb4-dfcadd348f81">remove</ac:inline-comment-marker> Capabilities from a Strategic Actual Deployment table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><div class="confluence-information-macro-body"><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="22fa6508-8c10-4c93-81de-77d2cdc66cab">Exhibits</ac:inline-comment-marker> </span>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="207dc2aa-a996-483c-bb42-d6b03dae58d9">model</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li><li>If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Phases relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To hide Capabilities in a Strategic Actual Deployment table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Actual Resources from a Strategic Actual Deployment table</h3><p>To <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d3010740-1c95-480c-b6d5-0c64e3fab6c2">remove</ac:inline-comment-marker> the deployment of the Actual Resources from a Strategic Actual Deployment table</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="45a44604-f9df-4ee6-98b2-497d1938cb5b"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="65485e66-45ee-441e-88f6-812b7abba19b">Select an</ac:inline-comment-marker> Actual Resource and click the</ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="45a44604-f9df-4ee6-98b2-497d1938cb5b"> -</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="45a44604-f9df-4ee6-98b2-497d1938cb5b"> button.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.</span> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="45a44604-f9df-4ee6-98b2-497d1938cb5b"><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="45a44604-f9df-4ee6-98b2-497d1938cb5b">Removing rows from the table</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="45a44604-f9df-4ee6-98b2-497d1938cb5b">To remove a row from the table</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="45a44604-f9df-4ee6-98b2-497d1938cb5b">Select a row you want to remove.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33d4780d-d26c-4c4d-aef3-14d9b95efbce"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the Strategic Actual Deployment table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_report.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af9a68f6-a0bc-4c55-9ee4-175d143d8233"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">228689575</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Logging background actions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721900 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01294: Working with Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `136721900`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721900/Working+with+Strategic+Actual+Strategic+Phase+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:46.835+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The** **Strategic Actual **Strategic** Phase Taxonomy Table (St-Tx)** specifies all the Actual Strategic Phases referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, the view can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and user requirements.

To create Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table, do one of the following

- Click the Add New button to add a new Actual Strategic Phase (or its specializations) instance.
- Click the Add Existing button to add the existing Actual Strategic Phase . Select the existing Actual Strategic Phase from the Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>The</strong> <strong>Strategic Actual <strong>Strategic</strong> Phase Taxonomy Table (St-Tx)</strong> specifies all the Actual Strategic Phases referenced throughout one or more architectures. In addition, the view can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and user requirements. </p><p><br /></p><p>To create Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Taxonomy Table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">to add a new Actual Strategic Phase (or its specializations) instance<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">.</span></span></li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button to add the existing<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Actual Strategic Phase</span>. Select the existing <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Strategic Phase</span> from the <strong>Select Actual Strategic Phase </strong>dialog.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721827 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01295: Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `136721827`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721827/Working+with+Strategic+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:42.606+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Strategic Connectivity diagram

1. Create or reuse Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Taxonomy' space='UAF12P2024x'] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Strategic Connectivity diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Connectivity and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Strategic Connectivity . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_connectivity_diagram.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Strategic **Connectivity** and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Actual Enterprise Phase element, but the same is valid for other Actual Strategic Phase elements.

To create an Actual Enterprise Phase in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Actual Enterprise Phase and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_capability_palette.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an Actual Enterprise Phase in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Strategic Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Actual Enterprise Phase and select it. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_capability_containment_tree.png']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_capability_to_diagram.png']

The structure of each Capability can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [Creating elements from other resources](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Creating+elements+from+other+resources).

##### Creating Strategic Exchanges

Before creating Strategic Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Resource Performer, Strategic Information, Geo Politic Extent Type or their specializations). The you can start creating Strategic Exchanges.

To create a Strategic Exchange in the Strategic Connectivity diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select an Actual Enterprise Phase and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Strategic Exchange. Click another Actual Enterprise Phase to connect. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_exchange_sm.png']
  - In the diagram palette, c lick **Strategic Exchange** and on the diagram pane, connect Actual Enterprise Phases. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_exchange_pallete.png'] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Strategic Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Realized Strategic Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Specify the Exchange Name, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Strategic Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Capabilities from <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Strategic Taxonomy" /></ac:link> (recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.</li></ol><h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p>To create a Strategic Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Connectivity and do one of the following:<ul><li>From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Strategic Connectivity</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="206" ac:width="325"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_connectivity_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Strategic <strong>Connectivity </strong></strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p>When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Actual Enterprise Phase element, but the same is valid for other Actual Strategic Phase elements.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Actual Enterprise Phase in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click the Actual Enterprise Phase and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_capability_palette.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Name the element.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create an Actual Enterprise Phase in a Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right click the Strategic Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Actual Enterprise Phase and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_capability_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_capability_to_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br />The structure of each Capability can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Creating+elements+from+other+resources">Creating elements from other resources</a>.</p><h3>Creating Strategic Exchanges</h3><p>Before creating Strategic Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Resource Performer, Strategic Information, Geo Politic Extent Type or their specializations). The you can start creating Strategic Exchanges.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Strategic Exchange in the Strategic Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select an Actual Enterprise Phase and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Strategic Exchange. Click another Actual Enterprise Phase to connect.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_exchange_sm.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li> In the diagram palette, c<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">lick <strong>Strategic Exchange</strong> and on the diagram pane, connect Actual Enterprise Phases.</span> <br /></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_exchange_pallete.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br />The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Strategic Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Strategic Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Name, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_strategic_exchange_wizard.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721835 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01296: Working with Strategic Connectivity matrix

- page_id: `136721835`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721835/Working+with+Strategic+Connectivity+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:42.801+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Connectivity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1283082281

1283082283

1283082282

The Strategic Connectivity Matrix (St-Cn) shows the direct and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024x']) dependencies between the Capabilities.

The Rows and the Columns of this matrix are Capabilities.

To build the matrix

1. Specify Rows scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify Columns scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.

Capabilities map to Capabilities using Dependency relationship.

To map Capability to Capability

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.

1283082280

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721839 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01297: Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `136721839`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721839/Working+with+Strategic+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:43.295+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Strategic Constraints Definition diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Constraints package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Strategic Constraints Definition . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_strategic_constraints_definition.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Strategic Constraints Definition and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Capability (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_capability_for_strategic_constraints.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the Strategic Constraints package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Capability (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).

##### Creating and applying Strategic Constraints

You can create the Strategic Constraints in the same way as other elements - from the diagram palette or in the Containment tree. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']. Also, you can create constraints in the selected Capability Specification window.

To create a Strategic Constraint in the Specification window

1. Select a needed Capability a nd open its Specification window.
2. In the Constraints property group, click Create > Strategic > Strategic Constraint .
3. Specify the name.
4. In the Specification cell, define the rule. The specification(rule) can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

Capability is a constrained element that can be a subject of the Strategic Constraint.

To apply Strategic Constraints, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, select a Strategic Constraint and drag it on the Capability .
- In the needed Capability Specification window, go to Constraints property group. Click the Apply button and from the Select Element window, choose a constraint.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create a Strategic Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="46a94466-ae14-4a0f-9c8d-69aa2911877b">Strategic Constraints</ac:inline-comment-marker> package and do one of the following:<ul><li>From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Strategic Constraints Definition</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="289" ac:width="309"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_strategic_constraints_definition.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram" the appropriate elements.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an element in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click the Capability (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="221" ac:width="577"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_capability_for_strategic_constraints.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.<br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an element in the Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Right-click the Strategic Constraints package and from the shortcut menu, select </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Search for the Capability </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(or another appropriate element)</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="332" ac:width="309"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_capability_from_containment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="339" ac:width="856"><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_capability.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating and applying Strategic Constraints</h3><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">You can create the Strategic Constraints in the same way as other elements - from the diagram palette or in the Containment tree. See <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating an element"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram" /></ac:link>. Also, you can create constraints <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="de19a3af-57ac-4dd7-9aea-e5dadffafe02">in the selected Capability Specification window.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p><p><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">To create a Strategic Constraint in the Specification window</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select a needed <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7a5df4f0-60dc-4383-9641-2ae3e451baa7">Capability a</ac:inline-comment-marker>nd open its Specification window.</li><li>In the Constraints property group, click <strong>Create </strong>&gt; <strong>Strategic </strong>&gt; <strong>Strategic Constraint</strong>.</li><li>Specify the name.</li><li><p>In the Specification cell, define the rule. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0e4674c-3d35-46c9-8132-9ab14424f243"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The specification<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> (rule) can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Capability is a constrained element that can be a subject of the Strategic Constraint.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">To apply Strategic Constraints, d</span>o one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Containment tree, select a Strategic Constraint and drag it on the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a460cd34-d834-4664-ba79-fa98ae8e7b95">Capability</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li><li>In the needed Capability <strong>Specification </strong>window, go to <strong>Constraints </strong>property group. Click the <strong>Apply </strong>button and from the <strong>Select Element</strong> window, choose a constraint.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721844 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01298: Working with Strategic Constraints table

- page_id: `136721844`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721844/Working+with+Strategic+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:43.461+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Strategic Constraints**table specifies constraints that apply to capabilities.

To create a Strategic Constraints table

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the**Select Subject Of Strategic Constraint**dialog, choose the needed constrained Capabilities.
3. Click **OK**.
4. In the**Rule****Specification** cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify the body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

To add an existing Strategic Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, select Strategic Constraints. The filled row is added to the table.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Strategic Constraints table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<span> </span><strong>Strategic Constraints</strong><span> </span>table specifies constraints that apply to capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Strategic Constraints table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add New </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">button. The new row is added.</span></li><li>In the<strong> Applies To</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Constraints table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>and in the<span> </span><strong>Select Subject Of Strategic Constraint</strong><span> </span>dialog, choose the needed constrained Capabilities.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the<strong> Rule </strong><strong>Specification</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Constraints table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>and in</span> the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, select the language and specify the body.<br /></span></span></p><p>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).</p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></p><p>To add an existing Strategic Constraint</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. </li><li>In the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, select Strategic Constraints. The filled row is added to the table. </li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the Strategic Constraints table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721902 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01300: Working with Strategic Motivation diagram

- page_id: `136721902`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721902/Working+with+Strategic+Motivation+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:47.004+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Motivation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Strategic Motivation diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Motivation and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Strategic Motivation . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_motivation.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Strategic Motivation and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. To illustrate the example, Opportunity and Challenge elements are used, but you can also choose Driver or Effect.

To create an Opportunity in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click Opportunity and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.
2. Name the element.

To create an Opportunity in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Strategic Motivation package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Select Opportunity .
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability.

##### Connect the elements

When you have Opportunity created, you can start connecting them using the Enables or Motivated By relationships. If you choose Enables relationship, the Enterprise Goal is created on the other end of the relationship. If you choose Motivated By, a Challenge is created on the other end of the relationship.

To connect the elements with the Motivated By relationship

1. Select an element from which the Motivates By will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Motivated By. You can also select the Motivated By relationship from the diagram palette.
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create an Strategic Motivation diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Motivation and do one of the following:<ul><li> From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Strategic Motivation</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="375" ac:width="331"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_motivation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Motivation diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Strategic Motivation </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. To illustrate the example, Opportunity and Challenge elements are used, but you can also choose  Driver or Effect.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Opportunity in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click Opportunity and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.<br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Opportunity in the Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Right click the Strategic Motivation package and from the shortcut menu, select </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Select Opportunity</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.<br /></span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Capability.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connect the elements</h3><p><br /></p><p>When you have Opportunity created, you can start connecting them using the Enables or Motivated By relationships. If you choose Enables relationship, the Enterprise Goal is created on the other end of the relationship. If you choose Motivated By, a Challenge is created on the other end of the relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Motivated By relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element from which the Motivates By will be drawn.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Motivated By.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc7b57ae-a1f8-48e0-811e-28d2602359f3"><ac:rich-text-body>You can also select the Motivated By relationship from the diagram palette.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.</span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721904 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 01301: Working with Strategic Motivation Table

- page_id: `136721904`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721904/Working+with+Strategic+Motivation+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-03-12T13:58:29.328+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Motivation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A**Strategic Motivation Table (St-Mv)** allows you to organize Strategic requirements (Enterprise Goals, Enterprise Objectives) in a tabular form. Because the requirements are text-based, using a table format for inputting requirement details is more convenient. This format resembles a spreadsheet, offering more space than the confined boxes in a diagram. In this table, each row corresponds to a requirement, while the columns capture various properties of each requirement. With this table, you can:

- Create new requirements directly in the table or import the existing ones from your model.
- Directly edit the properties of the requirements.
- Directly generate requirement reports, renumber the requirement IDs, or export the table to CSV or HTML format files.

To create a new requirement in the table

1. In the table toolbar, click Add New .
2. From the menu, choose the needed requirement type.
3. Add the requirement's name and text.

To create a new nested requirement

1. In the table, select a requirement for which you want to create a nested requirement.
2. In the table toolbar, click Add Nested .
3. From the menu, choose the needed requirement type.
4. Add the requirement's name and text.

To add an existing Requirement to the table

1. In the table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the **Select Requirement** dialog, choose the requirement you need. If you want to add more than one existing requirement, in the **Select Requirement** dialog, click **Multiple Selection**.
3. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A<strong> Strategic Motivation Table (St-Mv)</strong> allows you to organize Strategic requirements (Enterprise Goals, Enterprise Objectives) in a tabular form. Because the requirements are text-based, using a table format for inputting requirement details is more convenient. This format resembles a spreadsheet, offering more space than the confined boxes in a diagram. In this table, each row corresponds to a requirement, while the columns capture various properties of each requirement. With this table, you can:</p><ul><li>Create new requirements directly in the table or import the existing ones from your model.</li><li>Directly edit the properties of the requirements.</li><li>Directly generate requirement reports, renumber the requirement IDs, or export the table to CSV or HTML format files. </li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create a new requirement in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong>.</li><li>From the menu, choose the needed requirement type.</li><li>Add the requirement's name and text.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create a new nested requirement</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table, select a requirement for which you want to create a nested requirement.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click<strong> Add Nested</strong>.</li><li>From the menu, choose the needed requirement type.</li><li>Add the requirement's name and text.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Requirement to the table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Requirement</strong> dialog, choose the requirement you need.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d90d594-141c-446a-9652-aa753ccb43d1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to add more than one existing requirement, in the <strong>Select Requirement</strong> dialog, click <strong>Multiple Selection</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 01302: Working with Strategic Parametric diagram

- page_id: `136721847`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721847/Working+with+Strategic+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:43.846+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Parameters
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1992025934

1992025954

1992025944

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024x'] to constrain the properties of another Capability. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024x'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

1992025923

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="309b9b67-1e24-40fa-9243-c4dfb2fe59f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1992025923</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721856 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01303: Working with Strategic Phasing diagram

- page_id: `136721856`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721856/Working+with+Strategic+Phasing+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:44.441+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923155723

923155725

923155722

**On this page**

923155724

##### Creating Strategic Phasing diagram

When you choose to create Strategic Phasing diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']** opens.

To increase the diagram readability, use legends. To learn how to use legends, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024x'] chapter.

To create a Strategic Phasing diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the Strategic Roadmap Package and select Create Diagram > Strategic Phasing .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Strategic Phasing** check box.

To create an Strategic Phasing diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']** is disabled

1. Right-click the Strategic Roadmap Package and select Create Diagram > Strategic Phasing .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter. The blank Strategic Phasing chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show CV-3/StV-3/NCV-3 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

##### Adding Capabilities

To add a new Capability

1. On the Strategic Phasing diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . An empty line for the newly created Capability appears on the diagram pane.

To add an existing Capability

1. On the Strategic Phasing diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done

##### Adding Sub Capabilities

To add a new Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Strategic Phasing diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add New Sub Capability .
3. An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.

To add an existing Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Strategic Phasing diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add Existing Sub Capability .
3. From the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding Capability Provisions

To add a new Capability Provision

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Strategic Phasing diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Displaying all Sub Capabilities

To display all Sub Capabilities

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.
2. In the Strategic Phasing diagram toolbar, click the Display All button.

##### Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram

The capability name becomes [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024x'] when one or more sub capabilities are removed from the chart.

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all removed Sub Capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

##### Deleting Capabilities

To remove a Capability from the chart

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Capability from the model

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Deleting Sub Capabilities

To remove a Sub Capability from the chart

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Sub Capability from the model

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Editing Capability Provision

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

##### Removing Capability Provision

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

606447255

## PAGE 01304: Working with Strategic Processes diagram

- page_id: `136721899`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721899/Working+with+Strategic+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:46.755+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Processes
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Security Processes diagram for Security Processes hierarchies

1. Create Security Process
2. Create Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, or reuse them from the Security Taxonomy view.
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, and Security Processes.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Security Processes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Security Processes diagram for Security Processes hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Security Process</li><li>Create Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, or reuse them from the Security Taxonomy view.</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, and Security Processes.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Security Processes.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 01305: Working with Strategic States diagram

- page_id: `136721864`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721864/Working+with+Strategic+States+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:45.096+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1349294402

1349294405

1349294403

To create a Strategic States diagram

1. Create or select a Desirer (for example, Capability, Operational Performer, Capability Configuration)
2. Create or select an Actual State.
3. Create a Desires relationship between a Desirer and an Actual State.
4. Create or select an Achiever (for example, Actual Organization, Actual Resource) to achieve the Actual State.
5. Create an Achieves between an Achiever and Actual State.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7ec78ac-59b9-4f49-b1d1-e006c530b9ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349294402</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ef7afa0-a1e2-429b-9289-52772557d6a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349294405</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66ceaf01-71fc-45dd-bf5e-14fefa3515ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349294403</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a Strategic States diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Create or select a Desirer (for example, Capability, Operational Performer, Capability Configuration)</p></li><li><p>Create or select an Actual State.</p></li><li><p>Create a Desires relationship between a Desirer and an Actual State.</p></li><li>Create or select an Achiever (for example, Actual Organization, Actual Resource) to achieve the Actual State.</li><li>Create an Achieves between an Achiever and Actual State.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721867 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01306: Working with Strategic Structure diagram

- page_id: `136721867`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721867/Working+with+Strategic+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:45.338+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1242277357

1242277360

1242277356

**On this page**

1242277359

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Strategic Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Strategic Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Strategic Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Whole Life Enterprise element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Whole Life Enterprise in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Whole Life Enterprise and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To create an Whole Life Enterprise in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Strategic Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Whole Life Enterprise and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_whole_life_enterprise.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Whole Life Enterprise (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024x'].

##### Connecting the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Structural Parts (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Organization in Enterprise** and choose **Directed** **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic.str.change_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic.str.display_related.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024x'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic_compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. 
  1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
  2. Do one of the following:
    - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024x'] , and choose Structure .
    - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x'] .
  3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
  4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

1242283443

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9bee471-5e35-4e92-8a46-714337587da3"><ac:parameter
````
