ac:macro-id="fb1c04e8-1911-4a38-95a0-f49b8ab0a7c5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1242277359</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Strategic Structure diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Structure and do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Strategic Structure</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="202" ac:width="319"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_structure_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Strategic Structure </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Whole Life Enterprise element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Whole Life Enterprise</span> in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Whole Life Enterprise</span> and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="237" ac:width="484"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_whole_life_enterprise.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Whole Life Enterprise</span> in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right click the Strategic Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Whole Life Enterprise</span> and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="581" ac:width="351"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_whole_life_enterprise_containment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="222" ac:width="691"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_whole_life_enterprise.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Whole Life Enterprise</span> (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the Operational Performers</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_composition_palette.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="168" ac:width="907"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_draw_composition.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Structural Parts (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Organization in Enterprise</strong> and choose <strong>Directed</strong> <strong>Aggregation</strong></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="398" ac:width="339"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_select_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="188" ac:width="386"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic.str.change_role_name.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="660" ac:width="389"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic.str.change_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><h3>Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecb5d55f-6aa4-4cd0-80a3-94313a1f4d44"><ac:rich-text-body>If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the <strong>Resource Association</strong> also.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="864" ac:width="516"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_compartments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="510760c9-d0df-4fec-8215-ca1801ea4b29"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="713" ac:width="680"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic.str.display_related_nested.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Structure diagram" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c9928bb-8bd2-4e45-b832-52b0b2427d9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1242283443</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Smart manipulation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721883 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01307: Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `136721883`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721883/Working+with+Strategic+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:45.941+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1162351586

1162351588

1162365942

**On this page**

1162351587

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Strategic Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Strategic Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Strategic Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements.

To create a Capability in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Capability and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_capability.png']
2. Name the element.

To create a Capability in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Strategic Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Select the Capability . 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability.

##### Connect the elements

When you have Capability created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024x'] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

for <strong>Properties</strong>.</li><li>Search for <strong>Is Abstract</strong> and set it to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_is_abstract.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To view the inherited properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721892 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01308: Working with Strategic Taxonomy table

- page_id: `136721892`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721892/Working+with+Strategic+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:46.238+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Strategic Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button to add a Capability. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Capability dialog, select the desired Capabilities. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Strategic Taxonomy table</p><hr /><div class="wiki-content"><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button to add a Capability. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select the desired Capabilities. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721859 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01309: Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram

- page_id: `136721859`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721859/Working+with+Strategic+Typical+Deployment+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:44.754+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Strategy > Strategic Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

545192467

100.00002%

545192470

545192464

**On this page**

545192469

##### Creating Strategic Typical Deployment table

The context of the Strategic Typical Deployment table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Structure' space='UAF12P2024x'] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the Strategic Typical Deployment table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the Strategic Typical Deployment table are Actual Responsible Resources, and the columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Phases***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'].

###### emptyCreate a StrategicTypical Deployment table

To create an empty Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a Strategic Roadmap package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > Strategic Typical Deployment .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > Strategic Typical Deployment .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. A***Phases***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

To remove Capabilities from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Typical Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Phases***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Strategic Typical Deployment table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

627893252

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721145 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01310: Working with StV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `136721145`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721145/Working+with+StV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:52.040+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

412648820

412648822

412648819

**On this page**

412648821

##### Creating StV-3 diagram

When you choose to create StV-3 diagram, **StV-3 Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a StV-3 diagram from the wizard

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Click StV-3 Capability Phasing .
3. In the StV-3 Capability Phasing dialog, click Add . StV-3 Creation Wizard opens.
4. Follow the steps of the wizard.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the StV-3 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create StV-3”.

Also you can disable the wizard. Go to **Options** > **Environment**. In the **Environment Options** dialog go to **UAF** tab. In the General properties, set **Show CV-3/ StV-3 Creation Wizard each Time** 
**Creating New Chart** to false.

To create a StV-3 diagram manually

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Click StV-3 Capability Phasing .
3. In the StV-3 Capability Phasing dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram. You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.
5. Click OK . The blank StV-3 diagram is created.

##### Adding capabilities to StV-3 chart

To add a new capability

- On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add a new capability provision

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a capability provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the Capability Provision Creation Wizard .
4. Click Finish , when you are done.

To add an existing capability

1. On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding sub capabilities to StV-3 chart

To add a new sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Sub Capability .
3. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add an existing sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to add a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add existing Sub Capability .
3. In the opened Select Capability dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Removing capabilities from StV-3 chart

To remove a capability from the chart

1. Select a capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Remove From Chart .

To delete a capability from the model

1. Select a capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Delete .

##### Removing sub capabilities from StV-3 chart

To remove a sub capability from the chart

1. Select a sub capability. 
2. On the diagram toolbar, click **Remove From Chart**.

To delete a sub capability from the model

1. Select a sub capability. 
2. On the diagram toolbar, click **Delete**.

##### Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all removed Sub Capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

##### Display all sub capabilities in StV-3 chart

To display all sub capabilities

1. Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.
2. In the StV-3 chart toolbar, click the Display All button.

##### Editing Capability Provision

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

##### Removing Capability Provision

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4358bc7-680f-4d06-9424-cab6fdcae458"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">412648820</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4da63008-cc48-4266-95d6-94e4c0c71007"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">412648822</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4099659-8d09-4136-988b-5c50abbeb16b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">412648819</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2ee3a39-6b3a-47c7-a1e3-49669876886b" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cbbeacf6-4912-4ecf-b243-b63e049e3194"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">412648821</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Creating StV-3 diagram</h3><p>When you choose to create StV-3 diagram, <strong>StV-3 Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a StV-3 diagram from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>StV-3 Capability Phasing</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>StV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> dialog, click <strong>Add</strong>. <strong>StV-3 Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42bbac58-44fe-4e64-a652-17776450832f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the StV-3 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create StV-3”.</p><p>Also you can disable the wizard. Go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog go to <strong>UAF</strong> tab. In the General properties, set <strong>Show CV-3/ StV-3 Creation Wizard each Time</strong><br /><strong>Creating New Chart</strong> to false.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a StV-3 diagram manually</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>StV-3 Capability Phasing</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>StV-3 Capability Phasing</strong> dialog click <strong>Add</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24a1c224-731a-4774-a2f3-d3c6c6e0fc28"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The blank StV-3 diagram is created.</li></ol><h3>Adding capabilities to StV-3 chart</h3><p>To add a new capability</p><hr /><ul><li>On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add a new capability provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to create a capability provision.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add new Capability Provision</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong>Capability Provision Creation Wizard</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing capability</p><hr /><ol><li>On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select</strong> element dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Adding sub capabilities to StV-3 chart</h3><p>To add a new sub capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to create a sub capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add new Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing sub capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to add a sub capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add existing Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the<strong> +</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Removing capabilities from StV-3 chart</h3><p>To remove a capability from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Remove From Chart</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To delete a capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Removing sub capabilities from StV-3 chart</h3><p>To remove a sub capability from the chart</p><hr /><p>1. Select a sub capability.<br />2. On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Remove From Chart</strong>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To delete a sub capability from the model</p><hr /><p>1. Select a sub capability.<br />2. On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete</strong>.</p><h3>Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55f7247d-0bb5-4fe1-a13a-2815a70f9122"><ac:rich-text-body>The capability name becomes <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing Gantt chart properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[highlighted]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when one or more sub capabilities are removed from the chart.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To display deleted Sub Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.</li><li>From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Show/Hide Sub Capabilities</strong> and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click<strong> Select All</strong> to display all removed Sub Capabilities.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Apply</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Display all sub capabilities in StV-3 chart</h3><p>To display all sub capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.</li><li>In the StV-3 chart toolbar, click the <strong>Display All</strong> button.</li></ol><h3>Editing Capability Provision</h3><p>To edit Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Edit Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Edit the dates.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Removing Capability Provision</h3><p>To remove Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Remove Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721167 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01311: Working with StV-5 table

- page_id: `136721167`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721167/Working+with+StV-5+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:52.672+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413145832

413145834

413145831

**On this page**

413145833

##### Creating StV-5 table

The context of the StV-5 table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StV-1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the StV-5 table is Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the StV-5 table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.

Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.

There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To work with the StV-5 table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024x'].

###### emptyCreate a StV-5 table

To create an empty StV-5 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click an StV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. AnExhibitsrelationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] , select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Removing Capabilities from an StV-5 table

To remove Capabilities from an StV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An Exhibitsrelationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in an StV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Actual Resources from an StV-5 table

Toremovethe deployment of the Actual Resources from an StV-5 table

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select an Actual Resource and click the**-** button. Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the StV-5 table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

229836933

ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exhibits </span>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>To hide Capabilities in an StV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Actual Resources from an StV-5 table</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To </span>remove<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> the deployment of the Actual Resources</span> from an StV-5 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select an Actual Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.</span> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing rows from the table</h3><p>To remove a row from the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a row you want to remove.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the StV-5 table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="deployment_report.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with StV-5 table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f60e36a-1abb-4c92-820c-fd1f8175c4d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229836933</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721170 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01312: Working with StV-5 typical table

- page_id: `136721170`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721170/Working+with+StV-5+typical+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:52.865+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

627968607

627968609

627968605

**On this page**

627968608

##### Creating StV-5 typical table

The context of the StV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space='UAF12P2024x'] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the StV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the StV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the StV-5 typical table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 typical table' space='UAF12P2024x'].

###### emptyCreate a StV-5 typical table

To create an empty StV-5 typical table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a StV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. An***Exhibits***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a StV-5 typical table

To remove Capabilities from a StV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more StV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Exhibits***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a StV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a StV-5 typical table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a StV-5 typical table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the StV-5 typical table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

## PAGE 01313: Working with Summary and Overview diagram

- page_id: `136721822`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721822/Working+with+Summary+and+Overview+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:42.331+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Summary and Overview
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The page is under construction.

Please check the latest information at [https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP2024x/Working+with+Summary+and+Overview+diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP2024x/Working+with+Summary+and+Overview+diagram)

The **Summary and Overview** diagram shows a quick overview of an architecture description and a summary of the analysis. It serves as a planning guide in the initial phases of architecture development. Upon completion of architecture, it summarizes findings and any conducted analysis.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The page is under construction.</p><p>Please check the latest information at <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP2024x/Working+with+Summary+and+Overview+diagram">https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAFP2024x/Working+with+Summary+and+Overview+diagram</a></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The <strong>Summary and Overview</strong> diagram shows a quick overview of an architecture description and a summary of the analysis. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720833 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 01314: Working with SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `136720833`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720833/Working+with+SvcV-5+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:43.860+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create an SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create an SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with SvcV-5 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722837 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 01315: Working with Typical Measurements table

- page_id: `136722837`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722837/Working+with+Typical+Measurements+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:36.009+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1776767993

1776768014

1776768003

To create a Typical Measurements table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Add New button and from the drop-down list select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='UAF12P2024x'] . The new row in the table is created. Type the name of the Property Set and specify the Measurements of the selected Property Set.
  - Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Property Set to the table.
2. Click the Evaluate Measurements button to create the Actual Measurements table automatically.

In the Typical Measurements table, you can select one of the Display Modes:

- Owned Measurements - collects only owned measurements.
- All Measurements - collects all measurements hierarchically with primitive types.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The created instances are stored in the same location as the Typical Measurements table.

1776767992

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='UAF12P2024x']

The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span>Type the name of the Property Set and specify the Measurements of the selected Property Set.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button to add an existing Property Set to the table.</li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Evaluate Measurements</strong> button to create the Actual Measurements table automatically.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>In the Typical Measurements table, you can select one of the Display Modes:</p><ul><li><strong>Owned Measurements</strong> - collects only owned measurements.</li><li><strong>All Measurements</strong> - collects all measurements hierarchically with primitive types.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources_display_mode.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Typical Measurements table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="591b00b2-9507-4120-a97e-f2b7110f5b5a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The created instances are stored in the same location as the Typical Measurements table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7dcf5c7f-ff25-4ec9-bb53-124d65c3eb80"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1776767992</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Property Set elements for measurement tables" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
